linkedin_profile.headline: Six Senses London. Looking forward to supporting the U.K. and America geographical luxury feeder markets and partners.
linkedin_profile.about:
I love life and try and make all my relationships both business and personal long lasting. Live to love business development and making deals happen. Bespoke Sales & Brand Representation services for Luxury & Lifestyle Hotels and Resorts. I acquired and developed the right skills, relationship, networking and experiences necessary to support my customers, my actual goals and my continued improvement in the well known UK/EMEA and US markets. A highly driven Sales & Marketing person with a Tourism Management degree and more than 20 years experience working for well known, high end and new hospitality brands. Specialties: Luxury Sales & Business Development/ Hospitality / Travel / Corporate / High end Leisure / MICE / Networking / People Management. Known for driving improved revenue through understanding clients requirements and analyzing business and competitor trends. Account management; business development; global networking; creating and building strong relationship, teams and succeeding business goals. Goal driven individual responsible for managing over 300 Global accounts across multiple market segments, generating multi million $ revenue and maximizing market share and revenue from key feeder markets of UK/EMEA, U.S., South America. I am a firm believer in rolling up my sleeves and leading the way to get things done with the right team.

Total Experience: 17 years and 6 months

experience.title: Regional Director Of Global Sales - RSO EMEA
experience.organization: Small Luxury Hotels of the World
experience.description: New York, London, Paris, Rome, Zurich, Madrid, Tokyo, Bangkok, Hong Kong, Beijing, Singapore, Sidney, Jamaica, Maldives, Mauritius and many more top destinations. Head of Business Travel in the EMEA region: Affiliations, Global networking, Consortia, Mega Agencies, BTA and NAM for more than 300 key global accounts relationship for this prestigious portfolio of 520 luxury hotels carefully selected across 80 countries. Main focus: orchestrate brand-wide Strategic Plans to ensure optimal brand exposure and achieve a strong return on investment, i.e. active travel and trade show presence and detailed post trip follow-up; planning and execution of ongoing sales calls, road shows and client events/fam trips i.e. multiple hotel participation whenever possible together with EMEA VP sales Leisure team within the High end Leisure agencies and M&I networking activities; growth initiatives including direct sales targeting new global accounts; ensure alignment of individual hotel and company objectives and provide client/account profiles to the hotels as needed to increase overall lead volume and conversion of Global Sales leads to definite; ensure new properties are actively promoted to existing and new clients, leverage all brand initiatives to grow the new business. Brand Expert for assigned geographic region: maintains regional / segment key Consortia, BT agents and Corp

experience.title: Associate Director Of Sales
experience.description:
Responsible for positioning, sourcing, developing and account managing business from designated market sectors, such as UK/EMEA and U.S. high end Leisure and Corporate agencies and TTOO, with the potential for European markets (English, Spanish, French, Italian speakers). Responsible for driving sales from all main European and U.S. markets and developing and implementing strategy to maximize hotel revenue from affiliations and networking/consortia; determine, recommend and win prospective clients from market information and other resources. Management: deputizing for the Director of Sales, I support the setting targets, as well as managing direct reports and development planning. Supporting recruitment, induction efforts and annual budget setting and ongoing budget management. Responsible for overseeing all business development activities within the various named global accounts. Responsible for creating and implementing a strategy aligned with current global sales initiatives based on current and future growth plans as well as identify key contacts within targeted named accounts, solicit new business, identify and facilitate the RFP process and secure new business. Planning: Working closely with the DoS and contribute to forecasts, departmental budget and yield maximization; identifies customer requirements and expectations relative to regions and segments; uses resources and data to develop strategic plans to sell segments; understands IHG sales and business processes, supports corporate, leisure and M&I and hotel goals and contributes the enhancement of customer value. Communication: develops and delivers effective presentation (orally and written) where ideas, opinions, recommendations and conclusions are easy to understand; works with KADs whose end users work with assigned incentive intermediaries; establishes relationships and collaboration with IHG hotel partners.
Department: Sales • Level: Director

experience.title: General Manager
experience.description:
Company: 201-500 employees • Founded 2025 • Privately Held • Hospitality
Six Senses London will open with 109 guest rooms and suites and 14 branded residences offering access to the hotel’s facilities and all the privileges that come with a Six Senses home, including bespoke interior design, concierge service, dining, housekeeping, and wellness programming. There’s a cozy lobby bar and lounge (for talking to friends or the plants), an all-day dining restaurant with an open kitchen, and a seating area in the courtyard. Six Senses Spa will mirror the different energies of the city life, from the sensory stimulation and movement of the street to the stillness and calm of a quiet café or park. Highlights include 3,500 square feet (325 square meters) dedicated to fitness, a 65-foot (20-meter) indoor swimming pool, the Alchemy Bar, and a relaxation room with vaulted ceilings. On the second floor, residents and members will access a new kind of social and wellness club featuring a central bar and lounge, co-working spaces, a restaurant, and wellness and treatment rooms. It will offer pioneering programming to encourage growth, reflection and reconnection.
Department: C-Suite • Level: Manager

Securing strong digital and print press coverage across target media segments, encompassing features, profile placement, thought leader comment, news and product placements • Leading junior team members, setting high standards of excellence and ensuring consistent alignment to strategy and objectives • Inspiring innovative and engaging social media campaigns, influencer outreach and collaboration activity, to facilitate effective execution from the team • Developing strong collaborative client relationships with all key stakeholders

experience.title: Global Communications Lead, Hair Care

experience.title: Mentor Creative Mentor Network
experience.description: Providing young people with supportive careers guidance as part of a network of leading professionals from across the creative industries. The scheme brings together a dynamic community of mentors from varying paths and platforms. Creative Mentor Network vision: a society in which every young person can achieve their creative potential, irrespective of their start in life, opening creative industries to everyone. Training has encompassed an exploration of the barriers to entry for young people, unpacking theories surrounding diversity and inclusion, and exploring coaching strategies. www.creativementornetwork.org

experience.title: Global Lead Content Editor
experience.description: Leading Dyson's Global content agenda. With a specific focus on editorial, social and CRM, the role spearheads a new content model for Dyson, leading an agile, responsive and insights informed team to deliver high frequency, quality story-telling which drives engagement across all platforms. An extension of the Communications team, operating as a publisher/journalist, plugged in to cross functional teams (Marketing, Creative, Insights, Experience, Direct, Communications, Retail and beyond) to define and deliver Dyson’s editorial strategy and execute content plans. Working hand in hand with Dyson’s 35+ Markets to deliver inspirational content against a tightly defined set of global guidelines whilst leveraging regional and cultural insight. Responsibilities: • Ownership of Dyson’s global editorial calendar, ensuring synergy across all channels • Seeking compelling stories for Dyson, bringing them to life through varied content types • Identification and ownership of content partnerships • Integration of key narratives within NPD, evergreen and seasonal moments • Cross functional stakeholder integration to develop scroll-stopping content • Continually evolving Dyson's editorial approach based on key learnings and insights • Shaping the Dyson tone of voice and identity, setting the standards for premium execution • Upholding Dyson’s best practices in grammar, messaging, writing and style • Setting global guidelines and frameworks for Market execution • Defining formats, content stories and executions for emerging social channels expanding reach and engagement

linkedin_profile.headline: Director of Technical Services at The Whiteley & Six Senses
linkedin_profile.about:
I am an effective Director of Engineering with over nine years’ experience running maintenance operations and large projects at a leading London hotel. I am customer-focused and tenacious with experience across project management, team leadership, electrical engineering and waste management. I pride myself on excellent stakeholder management, workflow coordination and communication skills, managing large teams of internal staff and external contractors. I am an effective project leader and problem-solver with advanced programming skills and an ability to work with minimal direction to solve problems, resolve conflicts and respond to customer queries. Specialities: Project Management,Energy Management, Hotel Maintenance,Health and Safety & Compliance, Team Leadership, Customer Service.

Total Experience: 14 years and 2 months

experience.title: Mechanical Engineer
experience.description: In this role, I managed the repairs and maintenance of plant rooms, including air handling units, cooling towers and pool plant rooms, managing stock and ordering relevant parts needed for continuity of service with minimum disruption. I assisted the lead engineer with laundry equipment maintenance upkeep including boilers, washing machines, tumble drivers and automated machines, ensuing everything ran in line with expectations.

experience.title: Technical Quality Control Supervisor
experience.description: During this role, I maintained and updated an inventory of faults in the building, managing employees and contractors towards resolution at the highest standard, handing over rooms to hotel operations upon completion. I also developed the engineering department, negotiating a service agreement and supporting the Director of Engineering with the drafting and agreement of the yearly budget, ensuring sufficient flexibility for issue resolution.

experience.title: Assistant Director Of Engineering
experience.description: In this role, I managed the day-to-day operation of the 160-room hotel including guest rooms, spa, public areas, meeting rooms and plant rooms, managing 20 engineers across various tasks and ensuring tasks were appropriately allocated. Highlights from my role include: • Delivered training and development programmes for the engineers, ensuring relevant regulations were met • Undertook waste management and delivered an energy-efficient approach to running of plants and equipment • Managed the engineering budget and stock required to run the hotel with minimal levels of disruption

experience.title: Project Manager
experience.description: During this role, I planned and executed a £7m refurbishment project, converting the ground floor of the hotel from a restaurant and reception space into a new grand modern lobby space and library, as well as conversion of a members lounge and smaller serving pantry into a new all-day dining restaurant space with a central show kitchen. Highlights from my role include: • Led the tender process, analysing various offers and choosing contractors best suited to delivering a complex project, ensuring minimal disruption to hotel guests during the renovation period, as the hotel remained open • Liaised with the mechanical, electrical, plumbing and lighting consultant as well as structural engineers, architects and interior designers, in order to ensure development progressed in line with overall strategic objectives • Managed health and safety site compliance, ensuring that the refurbishment met all relevant regulations

experience.title: Director Of Engineering
experience.description: In this role, I manage a team of 20 engineers at the hotel, with an overall £2.4m budget, which is currently running £35k under. I this role I support team training and development, negotiating a £30k annual budget specifically for training courses. Highlights from my role include: • Improved the health and safety compliance percentage significantly from 77% to 91%, within a one-year period • Led a £250k project to replace the lighting for all 160 guest rooms, retrofitting new energy-saving LED lighting • Oversaw a £350k styling and artwork project in the guest rooms and public areas, from purchasing to installation

experience.title: Director Of Technical Services At Six Senses London
experience.description: I am happy to share that my role at The Whiteley Estate is expanding and I have joined the pre opening team for Six senses. I am so proud to work for companies like Savills and Six Senses who share very similar values.
